race.name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake
race.display_name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake
race.tagline: China's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series on the scenic 2022 Asian Games course at Thousand Island Lake

vitals.location: Qiandao Lake, Chun’an County, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China

climate.overview: Mild subtropical (15-25°C / 59-77°F in May), possible rain

terrain.overview: Mostly flat with elevation 100-326m; three categorized climbs: Cat4 (2.59km/3.7% @11km), Cat3 (3km/5.2% @30km), Cat4 (1.73km/6% @48km); well-paved lakeside roads through villages and countryside

logistics.explanation: Logistics score 3/5 due to the event's remote location in Chun’an County requiring travel to Qiandao Lake, with potential language barriers and organization by a Chinese team experienced in Dubai events but new to UCI Gran Fondo in 2025[1][4]. While the 2024 race drew 7,000 cyclists smoothly, international participants may face coordination challenges[3].

length.explanation: Length scores 4/5 for the 107km gran fondo distance on Sunday, ideal for serious amateurs, plus a 21km option, providing solid variety without extremes[4]. The 136km road race in the series shows commitment to substantial routes around the lake[3].

technicality.explanation: Technicality at 2/5 reflects the mostly flat lakeside roads with gentle hills on Chunyang Line, lacking demanding corners or rough surfaces despite minor climbs[1][3][4]. Riders focus on endurance over bike-handling skills in this scenic terrain[1].

elevation.explanation: Elevation scores 2/5 as the course stays low, varying from 100m to 326m with only moderate cat 3 and cat 4 climbs totaling modest gain on an otherwise flat profile[1][4]. This suits steady pacing rather than high-altitude suffering[4].

climate.explanation: Climate earns 4/5 for May timing in Zhejiang with mild spring weather, warm lake-influenced conditions averaging comfortable temperatures unlike hot summers or cold winters[2][4]. Lush forested surroundings enhance riding comfort[2].

altitude.explanation: Altitude at 5/5 given the sea-level profile around Qiandao Lake at 108m surface elevation, with course max 326m, posing no acclimatization issues for global riders[1][2][4].

adventure.explanation: Adventure scores 4/5 from looping north of Qiandao Lake's 1,078 islands amid 90% forested mountains, blending serene lakeside paths with climbs for exploratory thrill[1][2][4]. Submerged ancient city adds unique backstory[4].

prestige.explanation: Prestige at 3/5 as China's first UCI Gran Fondo in 2025, building on local 18th Qiandao Lake race with elites like Nicolas Marini, but still emerging internationally[3][4].

race_quality.explanation: Race quality 4/5 highlighted by record-breaking elite performances in 2024's 136km event with international winners and smooth execution for 7,000 riders[3]. UCI standards elevate the 107km gran fondo[4].

experience.explanation: Experience scores 5/5 for stunning lakeside scenery, lush mountains, and forested islands creating an unforgettable ride in Qiandao Lake's picturesque setting[1][2][3][4].

community.explanation: Community at 3/5 with massive local turnout over 7,000 in 2024 but limited Western presence, leading to language hurdles amid enthusiastic domestic cyclists[3][5].

field_depth.explanation: Field depth 3/5 features strong Chinese elites and some internationals like Marini and Blokhin in 2024, but UCI event's novelty limits deep global amateur competition[3][4].

value.explanation: Value scores 3/5 balancing premium UCI Gran Fondo access in a tourist hotspot with entry costs typical for international travel to remote Qiandao Lake[4][5].

expenses.explanation: Expenses at 2/5 due to high travel costs to Hangzhou's Chun’an County, accommodations in resorts, and potential currency/exchange issues for foreigners attending this isolated venue[2][4][5].

biased_opinion.overview: Historic first Chinese UCI Gran Fondo at stunning Qiandao Lake (1,078 islands). 2026's punchier 107km/1,800m course improves on 2025's flatter 136km. Qualifier prestige + tourist paradise = bucket-list event despite travel costs.

logistics.overview: Hangzhou Xiaoshan Intl (HGH) ~2-3hr drive. Time trial in Liangzhu (30min NW Hangzhou). North side Qiandao Lake start/finish. International field support expected.

history.overview: Founded 2025 as China's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series event and first international A-class cycling in Hangzhou post-2023 Asian Games. 2025 debut (Sep 6-7) drew 1,500+ riders from 30 countries as final qualifier for UCI World Championships. 2026 edition May 23-24 on 2022 Asian Games course.

course_description.character: 107km Gran Fondo starts north Qiandao Lake, big counterclockwise loop through lakeside roads, quiet villages, lush countryside. **Suffering zones**: Cat4 2.59km/3.7% (km11), Cat3 3km/5.2% (km30), Cat4 1.73km/6% (km48). Feed zone km60. Flat/fast 21km TT loop in Liangzhu (11m elevation).

non_negotiables: UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier

youtube_data.search_query: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake gran fondo cycling Qiandao Lake
